1.title: Navigate to Environment Safety Analytics Tab

content.text: The system shall allow users to navigate to Safety Analytics → Environment Safety tab.
navigate to the Environment Safety tab within Safety Analytics
I can view environmental safety analytics and monitoring data.
the user is within the Safety Analytics module
the user selects the Environment Safety tab
the system navigates to the Safety Analytics → Environment Safety view.
The system shall display the page title “Safety Analytics”.
the page title displayed at the top of the screen
I can identify the current analytics module.
the Environment Safety screen is loaded
the system shows the page title “Safety Analytics”.
The system shall highlight the “Environment Safety” tab as active when selected.
the Environment Safety tab visually highlighted when selected
I can identify the currently active analytics section.
the Environment Safety tab is selected
the analytics navigation tabs are displayed
the system highlights the “Environment Safety” tab as active.
The system shall load and display a 3D warehouse layout view upon selecting the Environment Safety tab.
a 3D warehouse layout view loaded when selecting the Environment Safety tab
I can visualize environmental safety data within the warehouse layout.
the user selects the Environment Safety tab
the system loads and displays a 3D warehouse layout view.

5.title: Display Last 24 Hours Safety Events by Default

content.text: The system shall display all safety events from the last 24 hours by default when the 3D warehouse layout is loaded.
safety events from the last 24 hours displayed by default
I can immediately review recent environmental safety activity.
the Environment Safety dashboard is displayed
the system displays all safety events from the last 24 hours by default.
The system shall display an interactive 3D representation of the warehouse layout.
I can visually explore the warehouse environment.
the Environment Safety view is loaded
the system displays an interactive 3D representation of the warehouse layout.
The system shall display warehouse elements such as zones, racks, pathways, and operational areas.
warehouse elements displayed in the 3D view
I can identify operational and safety-related areas.
the system displays warehouse elements such as zones, racks, pathways, and operational areas.
The system shall visually highlight zones using color-coded overlays (e.g., red, green, blue, purple). (on hold)
zones visually highlighted using colour-coded overlays
I can distinguish warehouse areas easily.
the system visually highlights zones using colour-coded overlays such as red, green, blue, and purple.

9.title: Dynamically Update 3D View Based on Filters

content.text: The system shall update the 3D view dynamically based on applied filters.
the 3D warehouse view updated dynamically when filters are applied
I can analyse filtered environmental safety data visually.
the user applies or updates filters
the system updates the 3D view dynamically based on the applied filters.
The system shall display a vertical filter toolbar on the left side.
a vertical filter toolbar displayed on the left side of the screen
I can quickly access environment safety filtering options.
the Environment Safety dashboard is displayed
the system displays a vertical filter toolbar on the left side.
The system shall display a Date Range filter dropdown when the user clicks on the date filter icon.
a Date Range filter dropdown displayed when selecting the date filter icon
I can filter environment safety data by time period.
the vertical filter toolbar is displayed
the user clicks the date filter icon
the system displays a Date Range filter dropdown.
The system shall display predefined options: Last 24 hours, Last 7 days, and Last 30 days.
predefined date range options displayed in the dropdown
I can quickly select common reporting periods.
the Date Range filter dropdown is displayed
the system displays predefined options including Last 24 hours, Last 7 days, and Last 30 days.
The system shall allow users to select only one date range at a time.
select only one date range at a time
only one analytics period is applied to the dashboard.
the Date Range filter dropdown is displayed
the user selects a date range option
the system allows only one date range selection at a time.
The system shall set “Last 24 hours” as the default selected option on initial load.
the Last 24 hours option selected by default
I can immediately view the most recent safety data.
the Environment Safety dashboard is loaded for the first time
the Date Range filter is initialized
the system sets “Last 24 hours” as the default selected option.
The system shall visually indicate the currently selected option using a checkmark or highlight.
the selected date range visually indicated
I can identify the currently active filter.
a date range option is selected
the Date Range dropdown is displayed
the system visually indicates the selected option using a checkmark or highlight.

content.text: The system shall dynamically update the 3D warehouse view and associated data based on the selected date range.
the 3D warehouse view and analytics data updated when selecting a date range
I can analyze environment safety data for the chosen period.
the Date Range filter is displayed
the user selects a different date range
the system dynamically updates the 3D warehouse view and associated data based on the selected date range.
The system shall allow the user to reopen and change the date range selection at any time.
reopen and change the date range selection at any time
I can switch between different reporting periods as needed.
a date range filter has already been applied
the user reopens the Date Range filter dropdown
the system allows the user to change the date range selection at any time.
The system shall display an MHE Filter panel when the user clicks on the MHE filter icon.
an MHE Filter panel displayed when selecting the MHE filter icon
I can filter environment safety data by MHE assets.
the vertical filter toolbar is displayed
the user clicks the MHE filter icon
the system displays an MHE filter panel.

20.title: Display Available MHE Assets in Filter Panel

content.text: The system shall display a list of available MHE assets within the filter panel.
a list of available MHE assets displayed in the filter panel
I can select specific MHE assets for filtering.
the MHE Filter panel is displayed
the system displays a list of available MHE assets within the filter panel.

21.title: Display Toggle Control for Each MHE Item

content.text: Each MHE item shall include a toggle control (on/off switch) for selection.
each MHE item to include a toggle control
I can enable or disable individual MHE assets for filtering.
the MHE Filter panel is displayed
the system displays a toggle control (on/off switch) for each MHE item.
The system shall allow users to enable or disable individual MHEs using the toggle control.
enable or disable individual MHEs using toggle controls
I can filter warehouse safety data for selected MHE assets.
the MHE Filter panel is displayed
the user toggles an individual MHE item on or off
the system enables or disables the selected MHE accordingly.
The system shall display the MHE name and type (e.g., Electric Forklift, Stacker, BOPT) for each item.
the MHE name and type displayed for each item
I can identify the available MHE assets.
the MHE Filter panel is displayed
the system displays the MHE name and type for each item, such as Electric Forklift, Stacker, and BOPT.

24.title: Enable or Disable All MHEs Using Master Toggle

content.text: The system shall provide a master toggle to enable or disable all MHEs at once.

the MHE Filter panel is displayed
the user uses the master toggle control
the system enables or disables all MHE items simultaneously.

scrolling support within the MHE filter panel

the MHE Filter panel contains more items than the visible area
the system supports scroll functionality within the MHE filter panel.

the user enables or disables MHE filters
the system dynamically updates the 3D warehouse view based on the selected MHEs.

the system visually indicates active (enabled) and inactive (disabled) MHEs.

the system allows the filter panel to be collapsed or closed.

52.title: Enable or Disable Heatmap Using Icon/Button

the Environment Safety view is displayed
the system provides a Heatmap icon or button to enable or disable heatmap visualization on the 3D warehouse layout.

53.title: Display Heatmap Overlay on Warehouse Layout

a heatmap overlay displayed on the warehouse layout

the system displays a heatmap overlay on the warehouse layout.

54.title: Represent Event Density Using Colour Gradients

content.text: The system shall represent event density/intensity using colour gradients (e.g., low → light colour, high → dark/red colour).
event intensity represented using colour gradients
I can distinguish low-density and high-density event areas.
the system represents event density and intensity using colour gradients, where lower density uses lighter colours and higher density uses darker or red colours.

55.title: Calculate Heatmap Intensity Based on Event Frequency

content.text: The system shall calculate heatmap intensity based on the frequency of events occurring in specific locations.
heatmap intensity calculated from event frequency
the visualization accurately reflects event concentration levels.
the system calculates heatmap intensity based on the frequency of events occurring in specific locations.

the heatmap updated dynamically based on selected filters

the system dynamically updates the heatmap based on the selected filters.

89.title: Respect Physical Layout Boundaries in FPV Navigation

content.text: The system shall ensure First-Person View navigation respects physical layout boundaries (e.g., floors, walls, rack geometry).

the system displays a Geometry Filters panel.
hierarchical filter options displayed in the Geometry Filters panel
I can filter warehouse geometry at different levels.
the Geometry Filters panel is displayed
the system displays hierarchical options including Site, Building, Structure, Zones, and Layouts (Racks).

101.title: Display Expand and Collapse Controls in Geometry Hierarchy

content.text: The system shall display expand/collapse arrow controls next to geometry elements that contain child elements within the Geometry Filters hierarchy.
expand and collapse controls displayed next to geometry elements with child items
I can navigate the geometry hierarchy efficiently.
the Geometry Filters hierarchy is displayed
a geometry element contains child elements
the system displays expand and collapse arrow controls next to the geometry element.
The system shall expand the selected geometry node when the user clicks the expand arrow icon.
I can view its child elements.
a geometry node contains child elements
the user clicks the expand arrow icon
the system expands the selected geometry node and displays its child elements.
The system shall collapse the selected geometry node when the user clicks the collapse arrow icon.
I can reduce visual clutter in the hierarchy panel.
the user clicks the collapse arrow icon
the system collapses the selected geometry node and hides its child elements.

104.title: Display Buildings Under Expanded Site Node

content.text: When the user expands the site node (e.g., “pune-ind”), the system shall display the list of buildings associated with that site (e.g., Building 1).
buildings displayed when expanding a site node
I can navigate site-level geometry hierarchy.
a site node such as “pune-ind” is displayed
the user expands the site node
the system displays the list of buildings associated with that site, such as Building 1.

105.title: Display Child Geometry Categories Under Building

content.text: When the user expands the building node (e.g., “Building 1”), the system shall display the child geometry categories under that building such as Structure and Zones.
child geometry categories displayed when expanding a building node
I can navigate building-level geometry elements.
a building node such as “Building 1” is displayed
the user expands the building node
the system displays child geometry categories including Structure and Zones.

106.title: Display Structural Elements Under Structure Node

content.text: When the user expands the “Structure” node, the system shall display the list of structural elements associated with that building, such as Walls and Floors.
structural elements displayed when expanding the Structure node
I can access building structural geometry.
the user expands the Structure node
the system displays structural elements associated with the building, such as Walls and Floors.
When the user expands the “Zones” node, the system shall display the list of zones defined in the facility layout (e.g., MHE Path 1, Aisle A, Aisle B, Aisle C, Dock).
facility zones displayed when expanding the Zones node
I can access zone-level geometry filters.
the user expands the Zones node
the system displays facility zones including MHE Path 1, Aisle A, Aisle B, Aisle C, and Dock.

108.title: Hide Child Elements When Parent Node is Collapsed

content.text: When the user collapses a parent node, the system shall hide all child elements under that node from the filter panel.
child elements hidden when collapsing a parent node
a parent geometry node is expanded
the user collapses the parent node
the system hides all child elements under that node from the filter panel.

109.title: Maintain Child Selection State After Collapse

content.text: The system shall maintain the selection state of child elements even when the parent node is collapsed.
child element selections preserved after collapsing a parent node
my filter selections are not lost.
child geometry elements are selected under a parent node
the system maintains the selection state of all child elements.
The system shall support multi-level expansion within the hierarchy to allow users to navigate through nested geometry elements.
I can navigate nested geometry elements efficiently.
the Geometry Filters hierarchy contains nested elements
the user expands multiple hierarchy levels
the system supports multi-level expansion within the hierarchy.

111.title: Select or Deselect Geometry Elements Using Checkboxes

content.text: The system shall allow users to select/deselect elements using checkboxes.
select or deselect geometry elements using checkboxes
I can control which geometry elements are displayed in the 3D warehouse view.
the Geometry Filters panel is displayed
the user selects or deselects a geometry element checkbox
the system updates the selection state of the corresponding geometry element.

112.title: Dynamically Update 3D View Based on Geometry Filters

content.text: The system shall update the 3D view dynamically based on selected geometry filters.
the 3D warehouse view updated dynamically based on selected geometry filters
I can visualize only the selected geometry elements.
geometry filters are displayed in the Geometry Filters panel
the user changes geometry filter selections
the system dynamically updates the 3D warehouse view based on the selected geometry filters.
The system shall allow the user to close the geometry filters panel using the x icon in the header of the panel or by clicking anywhere outside the panel.
close the Geometry Filters panel using the close icon or by clicking outside the panel
I can maximize workspace visibility when filters are not needed.
the Geometry Filters panel is open
the user clicks the close (X) icon in the panel header
the system closes the Geometry Filters panel
the Geometry Filters panel is open
the user clicks outside the panel
the system closes the Geometry Filters panel.
